title: Contract Testing and Advanced Testing
learningObjectives: Apply **Contract testing** with production-grade tests, tools, and measurable outcomes
Explain trade-offs between test types: unit, slice, integration, contract, e2e
Use profiling and benchmarking vocabulary correctly in senior interviews
Deliver behavioral answers with STAR structure tied to real architecture decisions
Run mock interviews with follow-up depth on testing and performance
Connect reliability practices from earlier phases to capstone scenarios

sections.title: Why Contract Testing and Advanced Testing matters
sections.content: **Contract** **testing** prevents **distributed** **systems** from **silently** **diverging**. It is a **senior** **topic** because it blends **API** **design**, **CI** **governance**, and **team** **boundaries**.

sections.title: Theory and Internals - Contract Testing and Advanced Testing
sections.content:
### Day 87 — **Contract** **testing**

### 1. Motivation
**Integration** **without** **full** **mesh** **e2e**.

### 2. Flow
**Consumer** **pact** -> **broker** -> **provider** **verify**.

### 3. Breaking changes
**Diff** **failures** **block** **deploy**.

### 4. Tools
**Pact**, **Spring** **Cloud** **Contract**, **WireMock**.

### 5. Culture
**Negotiate** **examples**, not **only** **docs**.

### 6. Story
**Field** **rename** **caught** **pre-merge** **via** **provider** **verification** **failure**.

sections.title: Basic — Contract idea
sections.output: consumer-driven contract: examples as executable spec

sections.title: Intermediate — Broker benefit
sections.output: broker stores pact json + verification matrix per version

sections.title: Advanced — Failure prevention
sections.output: can-i-deploy blocks if provider verification missing

sections.title: Pact flow
Exercise — Contract Testing and Advanced Testing (Day 87 assignment)
sections.problem:
Print **exactly** two lines:
1. Implemented: Contract Testing & Advanced Testing
2. Focus: Consumer-driven contract failure prevention.

sections.title: Interview Drill

conceptual.question: **Consumer-driven** **contracts**?
conceptual.answer: Consumer defines expected interactions; provider verifies—catches breaking API drift early. Senior answers separate **symptom** from **root** **cause**: **502** at **edge** can be **empty** **Endpoints**, **stale** **pool**, or **upstream** **panic**—each needs different **first** **commands**. Call out **ownership**: **platform** **SRE** for **mesh**/**certs**, **service** teams for **timeout** policy, **SRE** for **SLO** **burn** **alerts**—and how those teams **coordinate** during **sev-1** calls.

followUps.question: Production follow-up: which **dashboards** or **metrics** expose this for **Contract testing**?
followUps.answer: Start with **client** **outbound** **histograms** and **resilience4j** **circuitbreaker** **state** **gauges**, then open **distributed** **traces** filtered by **service** **name** and **route**. Consumer defines expected interactions; provider verifies—catches breaking API drift early. Explain **second-order** effects: **retry** amplification, **queue** growth, **thread** **pool** exhaustion, and **cost** of extra **hops** when **edge** dependencies like **Redis** rate-limit stores fail. Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads.

followUps.question: How do you **roll out** a policy change here without a **retry** **storm**?
followUps.answer: Use **feature** **flags**, **canary** **deployments**, **tight** **retry** **budgets** on **non-idempotent** calls, and **verify** with **load** tests that **match** **production** **fan-out**. Consumer defines expected interactions; provider verifies—catches breaking API drift early Tie answers to **Spring** reality: **Feign** + **LoadBalancer**, **WebClient** + **`ExchangeFilterFunction`**, **Resilience4j** registries, and **Micrometer** tags that make **on-call** searches fast. Explain **second-order** effects: **retry** amplification, **queue** growth, **thread** **pool** exhaustion, and **cost** of extra **hops** when **edge** dependencies like **Redis** rate-limit stores fail.

conceptual.question: **Pact** **broker** **role**?
conceptual.answer: Shares contracts and verification results across teams and CI pipelines. Communicate **executive**-friendly **impact**: **customer** **minutes** degraded, **error** **budget** burn, **mitigation** **ETA**, and **post-incident** **themes** mapped to **roadmap** work. Reference **security**: **mTLS** **service** identity, **OAuth2** **scopes**, **audit** logs at **edge**, and why **secrets** never belong in **Feign** **URL** query strings.

conceptual.question: **Schema** **evolution** **with** **contracts**?
conceptual.answer: Version fields, tolerant readers, provider verification gates on publish. When **Kubernetes** is involved, cite **readiness**, **HPA**, **NetworkPolicy**, and **Service** **DNS** as factors that change **latency** independent of **Java** **code**. For **gateway** and **service** stacks, emphasize **timeout** ordering (**outer > inner**), **bulkheads** per **tenant**, and **JWT** **cache** **TTL** during **issuer** rotations.

|---|---|
| CDC | Consumer drives |
